sections.heading: Introduction and Identification of Petition
sections.body: This response is submitted on behalf of Dr. Lena Vasquez in reply to the Request for Evidence (RFE) issued by U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services in connection with the above-referenced petition for O-1A nonimmigrant classification pursuant to section 101(a)(15)(O)(i) of the Immigration and Nationality Act and 8 C.F.R. § 214.2(o). The RFE requests additional documentation to establish that Dr. Vasquez has made original contributions of major significance in the field of machine learning. As set forth below, the record already before the agency—comprising a granted United States utility patent and a widely adopted open-source distributed-training framework—fully satisfies the regulatory criterion at 8 C.F.R. § 214.2(o)(3)(iii)(D). This response is submitted as a draft for review and signature by the attorney of record and does not constitute legal advice.

sections.heading: Original Contributions of Major Significance: United States Patent No. 12,114,778
sections.body: Dr. Vasquez is an inventor on United States Patent No. 12,114,778, which covers a novel method for distributed training of machine-learning models. The grant of a utility patent by the United States Patent and Trademark Office (USPTO) constitutes an official, government-level determination that the claimed invention satisfies the statutory requirements of novelty, non-obviousness, and utility under 35 U.S.C. §§ 101–103. This determination is made only after substantive examination by a trained patent examiner and, where applicable, after prosecution addressing the examiner's concerns. A granted utility patent therefore represents an authoritative and documented finding that the underlying contribution advances the state of the art in a meaningful and previously uncharted way. In the field of machine learning, distributed training methods are foundational infrastructure: they determine whether and how practitioners can scale model training across multiple processors, machines, and data centers. A patented advance in this space directly affects the capabilities available to researchers and engineers across the entire field. The existence of United States Patent No. 12,114,778 in Dr. Vasquez's name is not merely a credential; it is an independently verifiable record that she has identified and solved a problem in distributed machine learning that the USPTO judged to be non-obvious to persons of ordinary skill in the art. This constitutes an original contribution of major significance within the meaning of 8 C.F.R. § 214.2(o)(3)(iii)(D).

sections.heading: Original Contributions of Major Significance: The TENSORLOOM Framework
sections.body: Dr. Vasquez is the author and primary architect of TENSORLOOM, an open-source distributed-training framework that has accumulated more than 22,000 stars on GitHub and has been adopted by more than 60 companies. These metrics are not vanity indicators; they are objective, third-party-verifiable evidence of impact at scale. GitHub stars represent a deliberate action by individual users—engineers, researchers, and practitioners—who have reviewed a project and judged it worthy of tracking. An accumulation of more than 22,000 such endorsements places TENSORLOOM among a small fraction of open-source projects in any technical domain, and reflects the assessment of thousands of independent professionals that the framework provides meaningful value. More probative still is the documented adoption of TENSORLOOM at more than 60 companies. Adoption by a company—as opposed to individual endorsement—requires organizational evaluation, integration into production or research infrastructure, and ongoing reliance on the framework's correctness and performance. When more than 60 separate organizations have independently made that decision, the record compellingly demonstrates that TENSORLOOM has materially influenced how distributed machine-learning training is conducted across the industry. Dr. Vasquez's role with respect to TENSORLOOM is not peripheral or collaborative in the ordinary sense: she is the author and primary architect, meaning that the framework's design, its foundational architecture, and its continued development are the product of her individual expertise. The regulatory criterion does not require that a contribution be recognized through a formal award or citation count; it requires that the contribution be original and of major significance. TENSORLOOM satisfies both elements: it is Dr. Vasquez's original work, and its adoption by more than 60 organizations at scale constitutes major significance by any reasonable measure.

sections.heading: Convergent Weight of the Two Contributions
sections.body: Considered together, United States Patent No. 12,114,778 and the TENSORLOOM framework constitute two independent and mutually reinforcing bodies of original work, each of which independently evidences major significance. The patent establishes that Dr. Vasquez identified and solved a novel technical problem in distributed training at a level that the USPTO determined to be non-obvious and worthy of exclusive protection. TENSORLOOM establishes that Dr. Vasquez translated expertise in exactly this domain into a deployed artifact that the broader professional community has embraced at scale—22,000 individual endorsements and adoption at more than 60 companies. This convergence—a formal intellectual-property recognition on one hand, and documented real-world industry adoption on the other—provides the agency with multiple, cross-corroborating data points all pointing to the same conclusion: Dr. Vasquez has made original contributions that have materially advanced the field of machine learning. The evidence on record is not merely cumulative; each piece supplies the type of objective, third-party verification that demonstrates significance extending well beyond Dr. Vasquez's own assessment of her work.

sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.body: For the foregoing reasons, the record establishes by a preponderance of the evidence that Dr. Lena Vasquez has made original contributions of major significance in the field of machine learning, satisfying the regulatory criterion at 8 C.F.R. § 214.2(o)(3)(iii)(D). The grant of United States Patent No. 12,114,778 represents an official governmental determination of novelty and non-obviousness in the domain of distributed model training. The TENSORLOOM framework, with more than 22,000 GitHub stars and adoption at more than 60 companies, demonstrates that Dr. Vasquez's original work has achieved broad and documented industry impact. Together, these two bodies of work fully respond to the RFE's request for additional documentation of significance and impact. The attorney of record respectfully requests that the agency approve the petition and grant O-1A classification to Dr. Vasquez. This response is a draft for attorney review and is not a final filing.

disclaimer: This is general informational guidance only, not legal advice. Immigration law is fact-specific and changes frequently. An attorney of record licensed to practice law is required to review your petition and advise on your situation before anything is filed with USCIS.
